name: Marcus Johnson

activities.name: Varsity Soccer
activities.role: Team Captain (senior year), Starting Midfielder (all 4 years)
activities.description: 4-year starter on varsity. Elected captain by teammates senior year. Team went 14-3 and reached district finals senior year. Off-season: plays in competitive club league (FC Columbus Academy).

activities.name: Student Government
activities.role: Student Body President (senior year), Class Representative (sophomore, junior)
activities.description: Led school-wide initiative to extend library hours and add mental health resources to student handbook. Organized first-ever school-community town hall with Columbus city council member.
awards: Ohio Association of Student Councils Rising Leader Award (junior year)

activities.name: National Honor Society
activities.description: Coordinates chapter community service projects. Organized holiday drive that collected 800+ items for Columbus area shelters.

activities.name: Mid-Ohio Food Collective (Volunteer)
activities.description: Weekly shift sorting and distributing food. Has logged 400+ volunteer hours total. Recruited 12 teammates to join during off-season.

activities.name: Youth Ministry & Church Leadership
activities.description: Leads weekly youth group sessions (ages 12-15) at Shiloh Baptist Church. Planned and led two community service mission trips to Appalachian Ohio.

activities.name: Black Student Union
activities.description: Co-founded chapter sophomore year after recognizing gap in cultural representation at school. Organized Black History Month speaker series and cultural showcase events.

intended_career: Public policy, law, or community-oriented business leadership

essays.main_essay_theme: How building the Black Student Union required the same skills as captaining the soccer team — recruiting people who didn't initially believe in the project. Bridges athletic and civic identity naturally.
essays.supplemental_notes: Why Major essays are clear but somewhat general. Why School essays show genuine research on some schools, surface-level on others. Strong on schools where he visited campus.
essays.writing_style: Conversational, warm, confident. Authentic voice. Occasionally the structure could be tighter.

hooks: African American — URM consideration at many schools
additional_info: Father works in logistics management; mother is a middle school teacher. Family could contribute roughly $15,000/year toward college. Financially, public flagship or schools with strong merit aid are most realistic.

high_school.name: Westland High School
high_school.committee_context: Mid-tier Columbus suburb public school with solid AP access and honest grading. Grade distribution is not significantly inflated — a 3.85 UW represents genuine strong performance. ACT scores at this school correlate reliably with academic ability. Fourteen AP courses available; Marcus took 7.
high_school.gpa_adjustment_note: 3.85 UW is read at face value — strong performance at a school with moderate rigor and no significant grade inflation.
